issues.title: Run `watermark onboard sidney` to scaffold site directories and portable reach connectors
issues.body:
## Context
The onboarding review (2026-06-28) confirms that `sidney` is fully greenfield: every BOSC tool returns the `#424` non-substitution message, `data/extracted/sidney` does not exist, and no connector outputs exist for the site. The findings explicitly identify `watermark onboard sidney` as the single highest-priority blocking action.

## Grounding
From the findings (§ Prioritized checklist, item 1):
> **Run `watermark onboard sidney`** — scaffold per-site data dirs, set `corpus_relpaths`, and execute the portable reach connectors. Without this, every bundle feed inherits Lima or falls empty (the documented onboarding gotcha). *(Blocks all of §1–6.)*

## Acceptance Criteria
- `watermark onboard sidney` completes without error.
- `data/extracted/sidney/` directory is scaffolded and `corpus_relpaths` is set.
- Portable reach connectors execute and produce initial (possibly empty-but-non-null) outputs for all six subsystems (NPDES, grid, hydrology, GIS, corpus, hypotheses).
- No BOSC tool for `sidney` returns the `#424` non-substitution message after onboarding.

**Rationale:** This is the single action that unblocks every other subsystem gap identified in the review; nothing else can populate until it is run.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-sidney-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._

issues.title: Identify Great Miami NWIS gauges near Sidney and establish receiving-water 7Q10 baseline
issues.body:
## Context
The onboarding review confirms that every hydrology tool (`hydrology_balance`, `hydrology_scenario`, `sanitary_basis`, `stormwater_runoff`, `tier1_swmm`) returns site-scoped empty for `sidney`. The Lima reference build uses a 7Q10 low-flow screen against the Ottawa River; sidney's receiving water is the Great Miami system and Lima's hydrology constants must **not** be inherited.

## Grounding
From the findings (§3 Hydrology):
> **sidney status:** every hydrology tool site-scoped empty. No NWIS gauges configured, no 7Q10, no water balance, no stormwater run.
> **Gap:** configure `nwis_sites` for the Great Miami reach near Sidney, establish the receiving-water 7Q10, and run the portable reach connectors via `watermark onboard sidney`.

Also from § Status at a glance:
> Its receiving water is the Great Miami system, **not** Lima's Ottawa River; do not inherit Lima's hydrology constants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one USGS NWIS gauge on the Great Miami River reach near Sidney, OH is identified and configured in the site profile.
- A 7Q10 low-flow value for that reach is computed or retrieved and recorded.
- `hydrology_balance` returns a non-empty result for `sidney` using the Great Miami 7Q10 (not the Ottawa River value).

**Rationale:** The receiving-water 7Q10 is the hydrology spine; no downstream water-balance, scenario, or stormwater computation can be verified without it.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-sidney-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: identify-great-miami-nwis-gauges-near-sidney-and-establish-recei -->

issues.title: Identify Sidney-area WWTP NPDES permit IDs, ingest OEPA fact sheets, and run `watermark dmr`
issues.body:
## Context
The onboarding review finds zero NPDES/permit data for `sidney`: no fact sheets, no public notices, no DMR records. The Lima reference build has a multi-plant OEPA NPDES record (`data/extracted/oepa/`) covering American II WWTP (`OH0037338`), American Bath WWTP (`OH0023841`), and Shawnee II WWTP (`OH0023850`), established as the baseline pattern.

## Grounding
From the findings (§1 NPDES / permit profile):
> **sidney status:** nothing pulled. No fact sheets, no public notices, no DMR.
> **Gap:** sidney needs its WWTP NPDES inventory identified (OEPA permit IDs for the Sidney-area plant(s) discharging to the Great Miami), the fact-sheet/public-notice PDFs ingested under `oepa/` (Ohio jurisdiction), and `watermark dmr` run for the actual-vs-design flow record. Also the basin NPDES inventory (`watermark npdes --basin <miami>`) for the receiving reach.

The Lima DMR pattern uses `watermark dmr <id>` (e.g., peer Fort Wayne `IN0032191`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- OEPA permit ID(s) for the Sidney-area WWTP(s) discharging to the Great Miami are identified and recorded.
- Corresponding fact-sheet and public-notice PDFs are ingested under `data/extracted/sidney/oepa/`.
- `watermark dmr <permit_id>` is run and actual-vs-design flow records are available for the site.
- `watermark npdes --basin <miami>` is run and results are recorded for the receiving reach context.

**Rationale:** The NPDES permit profile is required to perform any effluent-load or water-quality analysis and to populate the site's regulatory evidence anchor.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-sidney-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: identify-sidney-area-wwtp-npdes-permit-ids-ingest-oepa-fact-shee -->

issues.title: Resolve Sidney's serving electric utility and EIA-861 number, including muni short-form check
issues.body:
## Context
The onboarding review finds no grid profile data for `sidney`: no `SiteProfile` grid knobs are verifiable as populated, and no `sidney`-scoped grid reference outputs exist. The Lima reference build uses `eia861_utility_number`, BA interchange (PJM-930), and an LMP/pricing zone. The findings flag a specific risk: Sidney may be a home-rule municipal utility requiring the EIA-861S short-form fallback.

## Grounding
From the findings (§2 Grid / utility profile):
> **Gap:** identify Sidney's serving utility and its EIA-861 number (watch the **muni short-form** case — Sidney is a home-rule municipal possibility; see the EIA-861S fallback precedent), confirm the **AEP/PJM** BA and the correct LMP zone, then run the grid/consumer-energy connectors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sidney's serving electric utility is identified and its EIA-861 (or EIA-861S short-form) utility number is recorded in the site profile.
- The BA (expected AEP/PJM) and LMP pricing zone are confirmed and recorded.
- Grid and consumer-energy connectors are run and produce non-empty outputs for `sidney`.
- The muni short-form case is explicitly documented as resolved (applicable or not applicable).

**Rationale:** Without the correct utility identity and EIA-861 number, grid demand and energy-cost analyses will either fail or silently inherit incorrect Lima values.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-sidney-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: resolve-sidney-s-serving-electric-utility-and-eia-861-number-inc -->

issues.title: Wire Shelby County OGRIP parcel/zoning GIS for Sidney after verifying situs city and state
issues.body:
## Context
The onboarding review finds no GIS data for `sidney`: no parcel/zoning connectors are wired, no footprint geometry exists, and no recorder deeds are present. The Lima reference build uses Allen County OGRIP parcel/zoning and recorder deeds (`data/extracted/recorder/`). The findings call out a specific hazard: "Shelby County" exists in multiple states, and the situs must be verified from a live sample before wiring.

## Grounding
From the findings (§4 GIS):
> **Gap:** wire Sidney's county (Shelby County, OH) parcel/zoning GIS — **verify situs city + state from a live sample before wiring** (the same-name-county-wrong-state hazard); OGRIP `County='Shelby'` is the OH parcel substitute. Footprint `parcel_acres`/developed/impervious stay `[open]` pending a recorded assemblage or stormwater permit. If no project site is yet identified for Sidney, this is a candidate-siting task, not a transcription task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A live sample from the Shelby County, OH OGRIP parcel layer is retrieved and the situs (city=Sidney, state=OH) is confirmed before any connector is wired.
- The Shelby County OGRIP parcel/zoning connector is wired in the site profile using `County='Shelby'` (OH).
- If a project site has been identified, `parcel_acres` and zoning classification are recorded; if not, the task is re-scoped as candidate-siting and tracked accordingly.

**Rationale:** Wiring the wrong county due to the same-name-county-wrong-state hazard would silently corrupt all parcel and zoning lookups for the site.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (/Users/cparent/Code/goedelsoup/bosc/data/research/onboard-sidney-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: wire-shelby-county-ogrip-parcel-zoning-gis-for-sidney-after-veri -->
